title: CDMGNotation

tex_statement.html:
Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG. We will write:

  1. $v \in G$ to mean $v \in J \cup V$,
  2. $v_1 \tuh v_2 \in G$ to mean $(v_1,v_2) \in E$,
  3. $v_1 \hut v_2 \in G$ to mean $(v_2,v_1) \in E$,
  4. $v_1 \huh v_2 \in G$ to mean $(v_1,v_2) \in L$,
  5. $v_1 \suh v_2 \in G$ to mean that either $v_1 \tuh v_2 \in G$ or $v_1 \huh v_2 \in G$,
  6. $v_1 \hus v_2 \in G$ to mean that either $v_1 \hut v_2 \in G$ or $v_1 \huh v_2 \in G$,
  7. $v_1 \sus v_2 \in G$ to mean that either $v_1 \tuh v_2 \in G$ or $v_1 \hut v_2 \in G$ or $v_1 \huh v_2 \in G$.

The star stands for a placeholder to mean: тАЬarrowhead or tailтАЭ.

lean.name: Membership
lean.code:
instance : Membership ╬▒ (CDMG ╬▒) where
  mem G v := v тИИ G.J тИк G.V

lean.name: tuh
lean.code:
/-- LN's `\tuh`: there is a *directed* edge `vтВБ тЖТ vтВВ` in `G`, i.e.
`(vтВБ, vтВВ) тИИ G.E`. -/
def tuh (G : CDMG ╬▒) (vтВБ vтВВ : ╬▒) : Prop := (vтВБ, vтВВ) тИИ G.E

/-- LN `vтВБ тЯ╢[G] vтВВ`: directed edge `vтВБ тЖТ vтВВ` in the CDMG `G`. Matches
LN macro `\tuh`. Unicode arrow is `тЯ╢` (`\longrightarrow`, U+27F6),
chosen distinct from the function arrow `тЖТ` to avoid any clash with
Lean's built-in syntax. -/
scoped notation:50 vтВБ " тЯ╢[" G "] " vтВВ => Causality.CDMG.tuh G vтВБ vтВВ

lean.name: hut
lean.code:
/-- LN's `\hut`: there is a *directed* edge `vтВБ тЖР vтВВ` in `G`, i.e.
`(vтВВ, vтВБ) тИИ G.E` (the same `G.E` membership, with arguments swapped). -/
def hut (G : CDMG ╬▒) (vтВБ vтВВ : ╬▒) : Prop := (vтВВ, vтВБ) тИИ G.E

/-- LN `vтВБ тЯ╡[G] vтВВ`: directed edge `vтВБ тЖР vтВВ` in `G`. Matches LN macro
`\hut`. Equivalent to `vтВВ тЯ╢[G] vтВБ` by definition. -/
scoped notation:50 vтВБ " тЯ╡[" G "] " vтВВ => Causality.CDMG.hut G vтВБ vтВВ

lean.name: huh
lean.code:
/-- LN's `\huh`: there is a *bidirected* edge `vтВБ тЖФ vтВВ` in `G`, i.e.
`(vтВБ, vтВВ) тИИ G.L`. Because `G.L` is required to be symmetric (see
`CDMG.L_symm` in `def_3_1`), `huh G vтВБ vтВВ` and `huh G vтВВ vтВБ` are
propositionally equivalent. -/
def huh (G : CDMG ╬▒) (vтВБ vтВВ : ╬▒) : Prop := (vтВБ, vтВВ) тИИ G.L

/-- LN `vтВБ тЯ╖[G] vтВВ`: bidirected edge `vтВБ тЖФ vтВВ` in `G`. Matches LN
macro `\huh`. Symmetric in `vтВБ`/`vтВВ` thanks to `CDMG.L_symm`. -/
scoped notation:50 vтВБ " тЯ╖[" G "] " vтВВ => Causality.CDMG.huh G vтВБ vтВВ

lean.name: suh
lean.code:
/-- LN's `\suh`: there is an edge with an arrowhead at `vтВВ` in `G`,
i.e. either `tuh G vтВБ vтВВ` (`тЖТ`) or `huh G vтВБ vтВВ` (`тЖФ`). -/
def suh (G : CDMG ╬▒) (vтВБ vтВВ : ╬▒) : Prop := tuh G vтВБ vтВВ тИи huh G vтВБ vтВВ

/-- LN `vтВБ тЗ╕[G] vтВВ`: there is an arrowhead at `vтВВ` (`тЖТ` or `тЖФ`).
Matches LN macro `\suh`. Unicode is `тЗ╕` (U+21F8, RIGHTWARDS ARROW
WITH VERTICAL STROKE) -- the vertical stroke evokes the LN's star
placeholder at the left endpoint. -/
scoped notation:50 vтВБ " тЗ╕[" G "] " vтВВ => Causality.CDMG.suh G vтВБ vтВВ

lean.name: hus
lean.code:
/-- LN's `\hus`: there is an edge with an arrowhead at `vтВБ` in `G`,
i.e. either `hut G vтВБ vтВВ` (`тЖР`) or `huh G vтВБ vтВВ` (`тЖФ`). -/
def hus (G : CDMG ╬▒) (vтВБ vтВВ : ╬▒) : Prop := hut G vтВБ vтВВ тИи huh G vтВБ vтВВ

/-- LN `vтВБ тЗ╖[G] vтВВ`: there is an arrowhead at `vтВБ` (`тЖР` or `тЖФ`).
Matches LN macro `\hus`. Unicode is `тЗ╖` (U+21F7, LEFTWARDS ARROW
WITH VERTICAL STROKE). -/
scoped notation:50 vтВБ " тЗ╖[" G "] " vтВВ => Causality.CDMG.hus G vтВБ vтВВ

lean.name: sus
lean.code:
/-- LN's `\sus`: there is *some* edge between `vтВБ` and `vтВВ` in `G`,
i.e. any of `tuh G vтВБ vтВВ`, `hut G vтВБ vтВВ`, or `huh G vтВБ vтВВ`. This is
the "adjacency" relation used in def 3.3. -/
def sus (G : CDMG ╬▒) (vтВБ vтВВ : ╬▒) : Prop :=
  tuh G vтВБ vтВВ тИи hut G vтВБ vтВВ тИи huh G vтВБ vтВВ

/-- LN `vтВБ тЖо[G] vтВВ`: any edge of any orientation between `vтВБ` and
`vтВВ` ("adjacency"). Matches LN macro `\sus`. Unicode is `тЖо` (U+21AE,
LEFT RIGHT ARROW WITH STROKE) -- the doubled stroke evokes a
placeholder at both endpoints. -/
scoped notation:50 vтВБ " тЖо[" G "] " vтВВ => Causality.CDMG.sus G vтВБ vтВВ

lean_explanation:
The Lean file equips the `CDMG ╬▒` structure from def 3.1 with the seven notations of the LN's `\begin{Not}` block. Throughout, `╬▒ : Type*` is the ambient vertex type тАФ matching the LN's silent assumption that the graph is over some carrier тАФ and `G : CDMG ╬▒` is the graph whose edges we are talking about; `vтВБ vтВВ : ╬▒` are arbitrary vertices.

Item 1 is registered as a Lean `Membership` typeclass instance, so the literal Lean expression `v тИИ G` typechecks and unfolds to `v тИИ G.J тИк G.V` тАФ `Set` union membership of `v` in the input nodes $J$ and the output nodes $V$ of the CDMG. Using the typeclass (rather than a bespoke predicate like `CDMG.mem`) is what makes the LN's literal `v тИИ G` write the way it reads.

Items 2тАУ7 are each a `Prop`-valued one-line `def` paired with a `scoped notation`. `tuh G vтВБ vтВВ` (LN: $v_1 \tuh v_2 \in G$) unfolds to set membership $(v_1, v_2) \in G.E$; `hut G vтВБ vтВВ` is the same with arguments swapped, encoding $v_1 \hut v_2$; `huh G vтВБ vтВВ` (LN: $v_1 \huh v_2 \in G$) is membership in $G.L$. The three 'star' relations `suh`, `hus`, `sus` (LN: $\suh$, $\hus$, $\sus$) are then defined as disjunctions of those three primitives, mirroring the LN's reading of the star as 'arrowhead or tail': `suh = tuh тИи huh`, `hus = hut тИи huh`, `sus = tuh тИи hut тИи huh`.

The accompanying `scoped notation` declarations install the visible arrows `тЯ╢[G]`, `тЯ╡[G]`, `тЯ╖[G]`, `тЗ╕[G]`, `тЗ╖[G]`, `тЖо[G]`, each at parser precedence 50. `scoped` means they are only in scope after a downstream file does `open scoped Causality.CDMG`, so files that do not open the namespace pay no notational cost.
design_choices:
**The `Membership` typeclass instance rather than a bespoke `CDMG.mem` predicate.** The LN consistently writes $v \in G$, so we register `v тИИ G` as a Lean `Membership` typeclass instance and the LN's literal phrase typechecks unchanged. A custom predicate `CDMG.mem G v` would have forced that explicit spelling at every callsite and would not interact with Mathlib's library of `mem_*` lemmas (`Set.mem_union`, `Set.mem_insert_iff`, тАж); with the typeclass instance and its accompanying `simp` defining equation, the full Mathlib `Set`-membership toolkit applies automatically.

**Six separate `def`s, not one inductive `EdgeKind` enum.** An alternative was a single inductive type `EdgeKind` with constructors for directed / reverse-directed / bidirected edges and one predicate `hasEdge : EdgeKind тЖТ CDMG ╬▒ тЖТ ╬▒ тЖТ ╬▒ тЖТ Prop`. We rejected this for two reasons. First, the LN treats $v_1 \tuh v_2 \in G$ and its kin as *atoms* in proofs тАФ for example, parents in def 3.5 are defined as $\{w \mid w \tuh v \in G\}$, and atoms compose better with `simp`, `rw`, and pattern matching than predicates wrapped around an enum constructor. Second, the three star relations are not single edge kinds but disjunctions of the primitive three; an enum would not represent them naturally, and we would end up writing the disjunctions anyway. Spelling them out directly mirrors the LN's own approach.

**`Prop`-valued, not `Bool`-valued.** Edges are propositions. This matches the LN, lets us write $(v_1, v_2) \in G.E$ directly (since `G.E : Set (╬▒ ├Ч ╬▒)` is already `Prop`-valued), and avoids imposing `DecidableEq ╬▒` at this layer тАФ later chapters of the lecture notes use real-valued (uncountable) vertex types, where decidable equality is unavailable. Where decidability is genuinely needed it will be introduced as a separate hypothesis.

**Unicode arrows distinct from Lean's overloaded short arrows.** For the three primitive edges we use the long arrows `тЯ╢ тЯ╡ тЯ╖` (U+27F6/F5/F7) rather than the short `тЖТ тЖР тЖФ`, which in Lean are already heavily overloaded тАФ function arrows, `Iff`, rewrite direction, and more. For the star relations we use `тЗ╕` and `тЗ╖` (arrows with a vertical stroke), where the stroke visually echoes the LN's star placeholder at the corresponding endpoint, and `тЖо` (left-right arrow with stroke) for the symmetric 'any edge' relation. Carrying the graph argument as a bracketed infix `[G]` keeps the notation unambiguous and sidesteps the awkwardness of trying to overload Lean's `тИИ`.

**`scoped`, not global notation.** All arrow notations live under the `Causality.CDMG` namespace and only activate after `open scoped Causality.CDMG`. A file that does not open the namespace pays no notational cost тАФ useful given how many Unicode tokens we introduce in one place.
